response:
Brittany uses 1/4 ounce of shampoo every other day, which means she consumes 0.5 ounces per day on average over a leap year of 366 days. To find the total amount needed, multiply the daily average by the number of days, resulting in a total consumption of 183 ounces for the entire year. This calculation helps determine how many [bottle_size] containers she must purchase to avoid running out before the year ends.

Since each bottle holds 14 ounces, dividing the total 183 ounces by 14 shows that she needs approximately 13.07 bottles. Because she cannot buy a fraction of a bottle, she must round up to the next whole number, meaning she needs to buy 14 bottles in total. You can find these products at your local [store_name] or order them online for delivery to your home.
